messages.content: You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Sou cloud architect e preciso fazer rightsizing de compute para TechCorp Brasil no projeto ecommerce-migration, em production/sa-saopaulo-1, considerando sem downtime. Qual abordagem voce recomenda?

**Analise de Custos — Rightsizing (TechCorp Brasil - ecommerce-migration)**

**Componentes de custo:**
1. **Recurso principal**: Based on usage/provisioned capacity
2. **Storage associado**: Volumes, backups, snapshots
3. **Networking**: Data transfer entre regioes e internet
4. **Servicos complementares**: Monitoring, Logging, Vault

**Otimizacoes recomendadas:**
- Use OCI Budgets para alertas de gasto
- Tag resources por projeto para chargeback
- Revise recursos nao utilizados semanalmente
- Considere Universal Credits para descontos por commitment

**Referencia:** [OCI Pricing Calculator](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/costestimator.html)

**Dicas de otimizacao:**
- Monitore utilizacao real antes de dimensionar
- Automatize start/stop de ambientes non-production
- Use OCI Cost Analysis reports mensalmente
- Avalie serverless (Functions, Autonomous) para workloads variaveis

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm
You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Como identificar recursos superdimensionados no OCI para DataFlow Solutions no contexto de data-lake-modernization, com cenario brownfield e requisito de sem IP público?

**Performance Tuning — Rightsizing (DataFlow Solutions - data-lake-modernization)**

**Metodologia de benchmark:**
1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais
2. Identificar bottleneck (CPU, memory, I/O, network)
3. Aplicar otimizacao isolada
4. Re-medir e comparar
5. Documentar resultados

**Ferramentas OCI:**
- OCI Monitoring: metricas em tempo real
- OCI APM: application performance monitoring
- OCI Logging: logs estruturados para analise
- Service Connector Hub: pipeline de observabilidade

**Comandos de diagnostico:**
```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \
  --compartment-id <ocid> \
  --query-text "CpuUtilization[1m].maximum"

# Verificar throughput de Block Volume
oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

**Referencia:** [OCI Performance Best Practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/performance.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm
You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
No ambiente staging da CloudNative Inc, qual e a melhor forma de analisar metricas de utilizacao para microservices-refactor quando o objetivo e troubleshoot e ha restricao de com budget limitado?

Guia de boas praticas para rightsizing - CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor:

**Planejamento inicial:**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de rightsizing
- Escolha a regiao `us-phoenix-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `staging` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**Fase de implementacao:**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: CloudNative Inc - microservices-refactor`
  - `environment: staging`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**Seguranca aplicada:**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**Operacao diaria:**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**Otimizacao continua:**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente



**Avisos importantes:**
- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm
You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Estamos em cenario de incidente e eu atuo como security lead. Como revisar capacidade provisionada para disaster-recovery-setup em eu-frankfurt-1 sem perder de vista com auditoria em 30 dias?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

Vamos configurar rightsizing para seu ambiente:

**Fase 1: Setup Inicial**
- Inicie no Console OCI acessando o servico desejado
- Selecione o compartment `shared-services` em `uk-london-1`
- Confirme que voce tem permissoes IAM adequadas

**Fase 2: Provisionamento**
- No Console, inicie a criacao do recurso
- Nomeie como `rightsizing-shared-services-004`
- Defina os parametros necessarios para seu caso

**Fase 3: Ajustes Finais**
- Ajuste configuracoes de rede e seguranca conforme necessidade
- Aplique tags para organizacao (project=RetailMax Online-compliance-audit)
- Configure monitoring e alertas conforme necessidade

**Fase 4: Verificacao**
- Teste acesso e conectividade
- Analise logs e metricas
- Registre a configuracao

**Referencia:** https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Qual desenho voce sugere para criar rotina continua de rightsizing no projeto network-segmentation da LegalDoc Manager, considerando review, mínimo privilégio e operacao em ap-sydney-1?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory

oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```

```bash
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Como automatizar rightsizing process no OCI para GameForge Studios no contexto de zero-trust-implementation, com cenario auditoria e requisito de com auditoria em 30 dias?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (GameForge Studios - zero-trust-implementation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

Como fazer rightsizing de compute no OCI para LogiTrack Logistics no contexto de security-hardening, com cenario incidente e requisito de integração híbrida?

Guia de boas praticas para rightsizing - LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening:

**Planejamento inicial:**
- Defina claramente os requisitos de rightsizing
- Escolha a regiao `ca-toronto-1` baseada em latencia e compliance
- Use o compartment `data-platform` para isolamento logico
- Documente arquitetura e dependencias

**Fase de implementacao:**
- Use Infrastructure as Code (Terraform recomendado)
- Implemente tagging consistente:
  - `project: LogiTrack Logistics - security-hardening`
  - `environment: data-platform`
  - `managed-by: terraform`
- Configure monitoring desde o inicio
- Implemente backup automatizado

**Seguranca aplicada:**
- Aplique principio de menor privilegio
- Use encryption em repouso e em transito
- Configure network security adequadamente
- Habilite audit logging

**Operacao diaria:**
- Configure alertas proativos
- Implemente runbooks para operacoes comuns
- Realize testes de disaster recovery
- Monitore custos regularmente

**Otimizacao continua:**
- Right-size recursos baseado em uso real
- Use auto-scaling quando aplicavel
- Considere preemptible instances para workloads tolerantes
- Revise configuracoes periodicamente



**Avisos importantes:**
- [MUTABLE] Precos variam por regiao e configuracao
- Sempre valide em ambiente de teste antes de producao
- Mantenha backups atualizados
- Documente todas as alteracoes

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
No ambiente analytics da MediaStream Pro, qual e a melhor forma de identificar recursos superdimensionados para multi-region-expansion quando o objetivo e review e ha restricao de mínimo privilégio?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Como analisar CPU utilization no OCI para TravelHub Platform no contexto de monitoring-overhaul, com cenario desativação e requisito de sem IP público?

**Performance Tuning — Rightsizing (TravelHub Platform - monitoring-overhaul)**

1. Establish baseline com metricas atuais

```bash
# Monitorar CPU e memory
oci monitoring metric-data summarize-metrics \

oci bv volume-performance get --volume-id <ocid>
```


You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.

Sou devops engineer e preciso migrar para shapes menores para FinServe Digital no projeto disaster-recovery-setup, em sandbox/eu-frankfurt-1, considerando com auditoria em 30 dias. Qual abordagem voce recomenda?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Como automatizar rightsizing process no OCI para LegalDoc Manager no contexto de network-segmentation, com cenario migração e requisito de mínimo privilégio?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

**Best practices:**

- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Estamos em cenario de brownfield e eu atuo como platform engineer. Como analisar storage utilization para zero-trust-implementation em eu-frankfurt-1 sem perder de vista com auditoria em 30 dias?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (GameForge Studios - zero-trust-implementation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.

Sou cloud architect e preciso migrar para shapes menores para MediaStream Pro no projeto multi-region-expansion, em analytics/ap-sydney-1, considerando mínimo privilégio. Qual abordagem voce recomenda?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (MediaStream Pro - multi-region-expansion)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

```bash
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \

  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**

3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
No ambiente sandbox da FinServe Digital, qual e a melhor forma de analisar CPU utilization para disaster-recovery-setup quando o objetivo e remediate e ha restricao de com auditoria em 30 dias?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (FinServe Digital - disaster-recovery-setup)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

Documentacao: https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Monitoring/overview.htm

You are an OCI FinOps specialist with expertise in rightsizing and utilization analysis. Provide technical guidance on CPU, memory, storage, and idle resource optimization.
Estamos em cenario de produção estável e eu atuo como sre. Como analisar storage utilization para network-segmentation em ap-sydney-1 sem perder de vista mínimo privilégio?

**Monitoring & Alerting — Rightsizing (LegalDoc Manager - network-segmentation)**

**Metricas essenciais:**
| Metrica | Namespace | Alarm Threshold | Severidade |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|------------|
| CPU Utilization | oci_computeagent | >80% por 5min | Warning |
| Memory Utilization | oci_computeagent | >85% por 5min | Critical |
| Disk Read/Write Ops | oci_blockstorage | >90% IOPS provisionadas | Warning |
| Network Bytes In/Out | oci_computeagent | >70% bandwidth | Warning |
| Database Connections | oci_database | >80% max connections | Critical |

**Configuracao de alarmes:**
```bash
# Criar alarme de CPU
oci monitoring alarm create \
  --compartment-id analytics \
  --display-name "high-cpu-analytics" \
  --metric-compartment-id analytics \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)

```bash
  --compartment-id sandbox \
  --display-name "high-cpu-sandbox" \
  --metric-compartment-id sandbox \
  --namespace oci_computeagent \
  --query "CpuUtilization[1m]{resourceId = '<instance-ocid>'}.mean > 80" \
  --severity WARNING \
  --destination-credentials '{"topicId": "ocid1.onstopic..."}' \
  --body "CPU utilization exceeded 80% for 5 minutes" \
  --repeat-notification-duration PT1H
```

**Dashboard recomendado:**
1. **Infrastructure**: CPU, memory, disk, network por instancia
2. **Database**: Connections, queries/sec, storage, replication lag
3. **Application**: Response time, error rate, throughput
4. **Cost**: Daily spend, budget alerts, anomaly detection

**Service Connector Hub — Pipeline de logs:**
1. OCI Logging → Object Storage (archive)
2. OCI Logging → OCI Functions (processamento)
3. OCI Monitoring → Notifications (PagerDuty, Slack)
4. OCI Audit → Streaming (analise em tempo real)

**Best practices:**
- Alarms com duration para evitar alertas de spike
- Escalar notifications por severidade (email → SMS → PagerDuty)
- Dashboards por equipe e por workload
- Revisar thresholds mensalmente baseado em baseline

**Referencia:** [OCI Monitoring](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/monitoring/home.htm)
